tasks.title: Generate country territories during world generation (Voronoi partitioning)
tasks.description: The world hierarchy schema already has geographic coordinate fields (mapWidth, mapDepth, mapCenter on worlds; position, territoryPolygon, territoryRadius on countries; position, boundaryPolygon on states; worldPositionX, worldPositionZ, radius on settlements) — but no generation logic populates them. Implement territory generation during world creation. (1) In the world generator, compute mapWidth/mapDepth from the configured world scale and number of countries. (2) Assign each country a position within the world bounds, spaced to avoid overlap. (3) Generate non-overlapping country territoryPolygon using Voronoi partitioning (or similar) so territories tile the world area with no gaps. Consider using d3-delaunay or a simple Fortune's algorithm — cell count is small (1-10 countries). (4) Territory shapes should be influenced by terrain — rivers and mountain ranges can form natural borders where possible. (5) Compute country position (centroid of polygon) and territoryRadius (max distance from centroid to any vertex). (6) Persist all fields to the database during generation. (7) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Place settlements within country territories with terrain-aware positioning
tasks.description: Settlements need to be placed at real coordinates inside their parent country's territory. (1) Use rejection sampling to place settlements at terrain-appropriate positions within their country's territoryPolygon — river settlements near water features, mountain settlements at elevation, coastal settlements at territory edges. (2) Persist worldPositionX/worldPositionZ to the database for each settlement. (3) Persist settlement radius from existing BASE_RADIUS in boundary-generator (hamlet=50, village=80, town=150, city=250, scaled by population). (4) Enforce minimum distance between settlements (sum of their radii + buffer) to prevent overlap. (5) Offset settlement boundaryPolygon vertices by the world position (local coords → world coords). (6) Generate inter-settlement roads connecting settlements within the same country — store as world-level street/road segments. Can reuse the existing RoadGenerator pattern. (7) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Subdivide country territories into state/province regions
tasks.description: When a country has states/provinces, its territory needs to be subdivided into non-overlapping state boundaries. (1) If the country has states, subdivide the country territoryPolygon into non-overlapping state boundaries using Voronoi or similar subdivision. (2) Each state's boundaryPolygon must fit within the parent country's territoryPolygon. (3) Each state's settlements must be positioned within that state's boundary. (4) Set state position to the centroid of its boundary polygon. (5) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Update Society preview map to render real geographic coordinates
tasks.description: The Society tab's LocationMapPreview currently uses arbitrary circular layouts. Update it to render actual geographic data. (1) World view: render all countries at their real position coordinates, with territoryPolygon drawn as the country border (replacing the circular border). (2) Country view: render the country's territory polygon as the border, with settlements at their real worldPositionX/worldPositionZ positions inside. (3) Settlement view: unchanged — already uses real lot/street positions. (4) Camera framing at each zoom level computed from actual entity bounds (bounding box of territory polygon, settlement boundary, etc.) instead of hardcoded radii. (5) Inter-settlement roads visible in country view. (6) Transitions between zoom levels animate smoothly. (7) Typecheck passes. (8) Verify in browser.

tasks.title: Update 3D game engine to use stored world coordinates for settlement placement
tasks.description: The 3D game currently computes settlement positions at runtime. Update it to read stored world coordinates so the game world matches the Society map exactly. (1) BabylonGame.ts reads worldPositionX/worldPositionZ from settlement data when placing settlements on the terrain. (2) Settlement radius from the database is used instead of recomputing. (3) Inter-settlement roads are rendered between settlements using the stored road data. (4) Terrain size is set from the world's mapWidth/mapDepth. (5) Player spawns at a position consistent with the world coordinate system. (6) NPC settlement zone centers match the stored world positions. (7) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Add world scale configuration to world creation UI
tasks.description: Allow users to configure geographic scale when creating a world. (1) Add worldScale config option to WorldGenerationConfig: 'compact' | 'standard' | 'expansive' or explicit { settlementRadius, countryRadius, worldRadius } overrides. (2) World creation dialog includes a 'World Scale' option with presets: Compact (settlement spacing 300-500, country radius 600-1000), Standard (spacing 500-1000, country radius 1500-3000), Expansive (spacing 1000-2000, country radius 3000-6000). (3) Custom option allows specifying explicit dimension overrides. (4) Selected scale is stored in generationConfig and used during world generation. (5) Typecheck passes. (6) Verify in browser.
